Research Title: Trust and reliability drive homepage conversion
Summary: Consumers trust online businesses less easily than before and strongly value reliability and transparency
Takeaway: UtahLister's homepage should feel reliable transparent and calm before it tries to feel clever

Research Title: Low complexity beats flashy novelty in first impressions
Summary: People form fast judgments and respond better to familiar low-complexity layouts
Takeaway: Use a premium but familiar service-page layout instead of abstract or overloaded visuals

Summary: Field experiment found better click-through when button wording repeated the same message as the headline
Takeaway: Make the primary CTA echo the hero promise instead of introducing a second idea

Research Title: Audience-first plain language is safer and stronger
Summary: Official plain-language guidance favors audience-centered clear wording over jargon
Takeaway: Use short direct customer-centered language and avoid self-focused copy in UtahLister messaging

Research Title: Do not rely on dark-pattern persuasion
Summary: Dark patterns can influence behavior but create trust and regulatory risk
Takeaway: Keep UtahLister persuasive but non-deceptive and avoid fake urgency or confusing CTAs

Research Title: Seller-owned account posting is the safest V1
Summary: Credential sharing and UtahLister-owned inventory listings create avoidable trust and security problems
Takeaway: Default to seller-owned posting plus UtahLister-guided posting support

Research Title: Real proof should appear early on the homepage
Summary: Specific proof stories outperform vague quality claims
Takeaway: Lead with Trek and Dr. Pen style proof instead of generic claims about optimization

Research Title: KSL workflow should follow platform-native norms
Summary: Official KSL guidance reinforces a standard account-and-listing flow
Takeaway: Do not overengineer KSL posting; support customer-owned posting flow first

Research Title: KSL trust features work best when the real seller is visible
Summary: KSL promotes verified profiles as a trust-building feature
Takeaway: Customer-owned KSL listings preserve the trust signal between buyer and actual seller
